quickFacts: Ranked #1 most liveable city in the world multiple years running
Coffee house culture is UNESCO-listed — don't rush your Melange
The €8/day public transport pass is one of Europe's best deals
Free open-air screenings at the Rathausplatz Film Festival in summer

places.name: Schönbrunn Palace
places.caption: Habsburg imperial summer residence
places.description: 1,441 rooms of Baroque excess. The gardens are free and enormous — climb to the Gloriette for panoramic city views. Book the Grand Tour for the full experience.

places.name: Naschmarkt
places.caption: Vienna's legendary food market
places.description: Over 120 stalls of cheese, olives, spices, fresh juices, and international street food. The Saturday flea market next door is perfect for vintage finds.

places.name: MuseumsQuartier
places.caption: Art complex & local hangout
places.description: World-class museums (Leopold, MUMOK) plus the best outdoor lounging spot in Vienna. Grab a drink and sit on the colourful courtyard benches like a local.

places.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral
places.caption: Gothic landmark with a mosaic roof
places.description: The colourful tiled roof is unmistakable. Climb 343 steps up the South Tower for the best view of Vienna's rooftops. The catacombs tour is delightfully creepy.

places.name: Prater & Giant Ferris Wheel
places.caption: Iconic amusement park since 1897
places.description: The Riesenrad ferris wheel has featured in countless films. The park itself is free to enter — pay per ride. The adjoining Prater park is great for running or biking.

places.name: Belvedere Palace
places.caption: Baroque palace with Klimt's The Kiss
places.description: Two palaces, stunning gardens, and Austria's most famous painting. The Upper Belvedere has the Klimt collection — arrive early for a crowd-free moment with The Kiss.

places.name: Donaukanal
places.caption: Urban waterfront with street art
places.description: Vienna's coolest strip — graffiti-covered walls, pop-up bars, and summer beach clubs along the Danube Canal. Strandbar Herrmann is the go-to sunset spot.

places.name: Hundertwasserhaus
places.caption: Quirky Gaudí-esque apartment building
places.description: Friedensreich Hundertwasser's colourful, wavy apartment block rejects straight lines. You can't enter, but the KunstHausWien museum nearby showcases his art.

places.name: Café Central
places.caption: Vienna's most famous coffee house
places.description: Freud, Trotsky, and Klimt all drank here. Order a Melange (Viennese cappuccino) and Apfelstrudel. The architecture alone is worth the visit. Expect a queue.

places.name: Alte Donau
places.caption: Urban lake for swimming & kayaking
places.description: Locals escape the heat at this old branch of the Danube. Free swimming, kayak rentals, and waterside restaurants. The Gänsehäufel beach has diving platforms.

places.name: St Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)
places.caption: Gothic landmark
places.description: Vienna's symbol since the 12th century. 137m South Tower (343 steps), coloured tile roof and catacomb tours.

places.name: Vienna State Opera
places.caption: World-class opera
places.description: Standing tickets from €4 — queue an hour before showtime for balcony spots. One of the world's great opera houses.

places.name: Hofburg Imperial Palace
places.caption: Habsburg residence
places.description: Sprawling palace complex — Imperial Apartments, Sisi Museum, Spanish Riding School, National Library and the Crown Jewels.

places.name: Prater & Riesenrad
places.caption: Iconic Ferris wheel
places.description: Giant Ferris wheel (1897) in the Prater amusement park. Night ride for twinkling city views. The park has free entry.

places.name: Viennese Coffee Houses
places.caption: UNESCO intangible heritage
places.description: Café Central, Demel, Sperl — marble tables, newspapers on sticks, Sachertorte and Melange coffee. A way of life since 1685.

foodScene: Wiener Schnitzel (veal, pounded thin, pan-fried) is non-negotiable — Figlmüller is the classic spot. Sachertorte at Hotel Sacher vs. Demel is Vienna's great debate. Coffee house culture is an event — linger for hours over cake and newspapers. Würstelstand (sausage stands) for a cheap late-night Käsekrainer.
